title: Intellia Files BLA After Lonvo-z Meets Phase 3 Endpoints
text: Intellia Therapeutics filed a Biologics License Application (BLA) with the FDA after lonvo-z met all Phase 3 endpoints in the HAELO trial for hereditary angioedema. The Phase 3 trial demonstrated an 87% reduction in hereditary angioedema attacks compared to placebo, with a favorable safety profile. 62% of lonvo-z patients were attack-free versus 11% on placebo. Safety was favorable, with only mild to moderate adverse events and no serious events reported in the treatment arm. A total of 80 patients were enrolled, with 52 receiving lonvo-z and 28 receiving placebo. Intellia initiated a rolling BLA submission to the FDA and is preparing for a potential U.S. launch of lonvo-z in the first half of 2027. The rolling BLA allows Intellia to submit portions of the BLA on an ongoing basis, providing the FDA with an opportunity to expedite its review.
